---
title: Human Microbiome Project
alias: &ALIAS humanmicrobiome
href: https://hmpdacc.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Human Microbiome Project (HMP) provides comprehensive datasets characterizing the human microbiome and its role in health and disease, including metagenomic, metatranscriptomic, and metabolomic data.

## Key Features

- Generated reference catalogs of microbiomes from 18 body sites across healthy adults (HMP1)
- Expanded to disease cohorts including IBD, type 2 diabetes, and preterm birth (iHMP/HMP2)
- Provides 16S amplicon, whole metagenome shotgun, metatranscriptomic, and metabolomic data
- Standardized sample collection, processing, and analysis protocols across all sites
- Freely accessible via the HMP Data Analysis and Coordination Center (DACC) portal

## Use Cases in Research

- Establishing baseline microbiome compositions for comparison with infection or AMR studies
- Identifying microbial taxa and genes associated with disease versus healthy states
- Integrating multi-omic HMP datasets for microbiome-host interaction modeling
- Training and validating machine learning models for microbiome-based disease prediction
- Benchmarking 16S and metagenomics analysis pipelines against well-characterized reference samples

## Getting Started

Visit [Human Microbiome Project](https://hmpdacc.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
